content.claim: Teacher shortage in Hungary is driven by low pay (among Europe's lowest), the highest student-teacher ratio, and insufficient support staff; Hungary committed to raising average teacher salary to 80% of tertiary-educated worker salary by 2025 and achieved a 21.2% average raise from January 2025, but no source quantifies whether this addresses specialist (as opposed to generalist classroom-teacher) shortages.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Salary investment addresses generalist teacher shortage but does not quantify specialist capacity gains (doctors, sign language interpreters)
No baseline or interim assessment of specialist shortage magnitude provided
Unclear whether salary levels alone will reverse composition and retention effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
